
```{r}
#install.packages("archive")
```


```{r}
# =========================================================
# Data Download and Preparation Script
# This script downloads all required datasets from OSF,
# extracts ZIP archives, and retains only the relevant files
# for the empirical analysis.
# =========================================================

# Create directory for raw data (if not already existing)
dir.create("data_raw", showWarnings = FALSE)

# =========================================================
# 1. Download individual datasets from OSF
# =========================================================

files <- list(
  study3 = list(
    url = "https://osf.io/sd643/download",
    dest = "data_raw/Study_3_data.csv"
  ),
  study4 = list(
    url = "https://osf.io/5ta42/download",
    dest = "data_raw/Study_4_data.csv"
  ),
  study5 = list(
    url = "https://osf.io/nu3qp/download",
    dest = "data_raw/Study_5_data.csv"
  ),
  trustworthiness = list(
    url = "https://osf.io/39s8a/download",
    dest = "data_raw/trustworthiness_rating.dta"
  )
)

# Download each file only if it does not already exist locally
for (f in files) {
  if (!file.exists(f$dest)) {
    download.file(f$url, destfile = f$dest, mode = "wb")
  }
}

# =========================================================
# 2. Download and extract ZIP archive (TFC dataset)
# =========================================================

zip_url  <- "https://osf.io/qwucj/download"
zip_path <- "data_raw/TFC_April2023.zip"

# Increase timeout for large file downloads
options(timeout = 150)

# Download ZIP file only if it does not exist
if (!file.exists(zip_path)) {
  download.file(zip_url, destfile = zip_path, mode = "wb", method = "libcurl")
}

# Load archive package for robust extraction
library(archive)

# Define extraction directory
unzipped_dir <- "data_raw/TFC_April2023"

# Extract ZIP only if it has not been extracted before
if (!dir.exists(unzipped_dir)) {
  dir.create(unzipped_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)
  archive_extract(zip_path, dir = unzipped_dir)
}

# =========================================================
# 3. Select only relevant files from extracted archive
# =========================================================

target_path <- "data_raw"

# List all files recursively in extracted directory
all_files <- list.files(unzipped_dir, recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)

# Keep only actual files (exclude directories)
all_files <- all_files[file.info(all_files)$isdir == FALSE]

# Define patterns for relevant meta-analysis files
patterns <- c(
  "Exp1_Share_Meta\\.csv",
  "Exp2_Share_Meta\\.csv",
  "Exp3_Share_Meta\\.csv",
  "1_Share_Meta\\.csv"
)

# Filter files matching the specified patterns
selected_files <- all_files[
  grepl(paste(patterns, collapse = "|"), all_files)
]

# Copy selected files into the main data directory
for (f in selected_files) {
  file.copy(f, file.path(target_path, basename(f)), overwrite = TRUE)
}

# =========================================================
# 4. Clean up intermediate files
# =========================================================

# Remove extracted directory to keep workspace clean
unlink(unzipped_dir, recursive = TRUE, force = TRUE)

# Remove ZIP archive after extraction
unlink(zip_path, force = TRUE)
```
